methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea is a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Christopher Amidon. The firm has raised $10M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of three private peers operating in the same sector—Clippership, NetworkOcean, and Albacore—with no public market comps available for direct benchmarking.

Given the limited methodological tractability at the seed stage and absence of public comparables, valuation relies on stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $25M. This represents the median point in the triangulated range.

The analysis supports a valuation band of $21M to $29M, with a central estimate of $25M. Material uncertainties attach to this range: the single-method foundation reflects limited data availability at seed stage; private peer financials remain opaque; and the 2.5x step-up multiplier assumes Standard Subsea follows normalized progression patterns. Additional diligence on burn rate, runway, product-market fit indicators, and relative positioning versus named peers would sharpen conviction. Any material follow-on funding or revenue inflection would warrant re-examination.
